Title: Generation of blue chrysanthemums by anthocyanin B-ring hydroxylation and glucosylation and its coloration mechanism

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Taihei; Sei Arabella; Sei Shawl; 940-0765; T27; T37; T57
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Blue delphinidin-based anthocyanins / blue flower color
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RNAi-mediated down-regulation of endogenous CmF3'H was tested to reduce cyanidin-derived red pigmentation, but was not essential for blue flower generation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic RNAi construct in pB428; flower color observation; anthocyanin profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. An RNAi cassette targeting chrysanthemum F3'H was included to reduce red cyanidin derivatives. 2. The construct was supplementary rather than essential for blue flower formation. 3. Blue flowers were achieved mainly by coexpression of CamF3'5'H and CtA3'5'GT.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Weakly acidic petal pH (~5.6) relevant to coloration
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used as an auxiliary module to suppress competing cyanidin branch, but not required for the target blue trait.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included only in pB428; discussion states this RNAi construct was not essential for generating blue flowers.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for delphinidin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Dihydroflavonoid anthocyanin precursors in the cyanidin branch (strongly inferable as dihydrokaempferol/dihydroquercetin-type substrates)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 3',5'-hydroxylated delphinidin-pathway precursors leading to delphinidin-based anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces hydroxyl groups at the 3' and 5' positions of the flavonoid B-ring, redirecting anthocyanin biosynthesis toward delphinidin derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate branch leading to target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 3',5'-glucosylated delphinidin-based anthocyanins associated with blue flower color
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Blue flower coloration in ornamental flowers
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of delphinidin-based anthocyanins; expression alone shifts chrysanthemum flowers from pink toward purple/violet but is insufficient for true blue color.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic coexpression and single-expression comparisons; qRT-PCR; anthocyanin LC-PDA-MS/HPLC profiling; flower color measurements
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CamF3'5'H expression correlated with delphinidin-based anthocyanin accumulation in transgenic chrysanthemum petals. 2. Lines expressing only CamF3'5'H accumulated non-3',5'-glucosylated delphinidin anthocyanins (A5/A6) and showed purple coloration. 3. Coexpression with CtA3'5'GT enabled formation of 3',5'-glucosylated delphinidin anthocyanins (A7-A9) and blue flower color. 4. CamF3'5'H also led to production of new flavonoids such as C2.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in ray florets/petals of transgenic lines; transcript abundance correlated with delphinidin content
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Weakly acidic petal juice pH (~5.6) influences final visible color; in planta copigmentation required for full blue phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Expression alone generates purple rather than blue flowers; may produce additional flavonoids such as tricetin derivative C2
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential module for introducing delphinidin branch into non-blue ornamentals; should be stacked with CtA3'5'GT and compatible endogenous copigments for robust blue color engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Both transgenes were driven by the chrysanthemum F3H promoter. The paper explicitly describes F3'5'H as the key enzyme for delphinidin biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for target blue-pigment formation in this engineering design
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Cyanidin- and delphinidin-based anthocyanins carrying free 3' and/or 5' hydroxyl groups
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 3'- and/or 3',5'-glucosylated anthocyanins, including preternatin C5 (A7) and ternatin C5 (A8)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to the 3' and/or 5' hydroxyl groups on the anthocyanin B-ring.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: 3',5'-glucosylated delphinidin-based anthocyanins associated with blue flower color
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Blue flower coloration in ornamental flowers
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables B-ring glucosylation of anthocyanins; when combined with CamF3'5'H, produces 3',5'-diglucosylated delphinidin anthocyanins and true blue chrysanthemum flowers.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transformation with CtA3'5'GT alone or coexpressed with CamF3'5'H; qRT-PCR; LC-PDA-MS/HPLC structural analysis; flower color measurement
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtA3'5'GT alone did not generate bluish flowers in chrysanthemum. 2. CtA3'5'GT expression correlated with accumulation of 3'- and/or 5'-glucosylated anthocyanins. 3. The enzyme efficiently glycosylated both cyanidin- and delphinidin-based anthocyanins in transgenic chrysanthemums. 4. Coexpression with CamF3'5'H generated A7-A9, especially ternatin C5-type pigments, enabling blue coloration through copigmentation with endogenous flavone glucosides.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in ray florets/petals of transgenic lines; transcript abundance correlated with 3'/3',5'-glucosylated anthocyanin content
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Weakly acidic petal juice pH (~5.6) and endogenous flavone copigments are required for the final blue phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Expression alone mainly produces 3'-glucosylated anthocyanins and does not yield blue color without delphinidin branch introduction
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical second engineering module for blue-flower creation; broadly applicable in species already containing flavone glucosides or after stacking with F3'5'H.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper reports that newly synthesized 3',5'-diglucosylated delphinidin anthocyanins are violet in weakly acidic solution and become blue in planta through copigmentation.
